title: MCP Architecture, Negotiation, and Trust Boundaries · Skill Brief: Trust Boundaries
summary: Trace one tool call across host, client, server, and external system. Planned artifact: Add and verify the agent ext trust boundaries increment in an MCP architecture and threat-boundary diagram. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map permissions, data flow, instruction authority, and side effects at each boundary.
Advertise and negotiate only implemented protocol capabilities.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Trust Boundaries
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: agent ext mcp architecture. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map permissions, data flow, instruction authority, and side effects at each boundary.

content.text: Map permissions, data flow, instruction authority, and side effects at each boundary. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern

steps.title: Defend the Trust Boundaries model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Trust Boundaries applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Map permissions, data flow, instruction authority, and side effects at each boundary.
